content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track queued, running, settling, canceled, failed, and completed jobs with exactly one owner and one terminal result.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Worker Ownership model · Theory Feed Ts Worker O…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 1360 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove multiresource budgets, owned workers, origin fairness, rejection isolation, and drain; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track queued, running, settling, canceled, failed, and completed jobs with exactly one owner and one terminal result.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Worker Ownership model · Theory Feed Ts Worker O…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An array of Promises is a sufficient worker registry.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-ts-worker-ownership-d0ck89(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_feed_ts_worker_ownership_d0ck89\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?workerJobs < 12)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?settledJobs < 12 \|\| settledJobs > workerJobs)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?originCases < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unhandledRejections > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
